administrativeDirectives.excerpt: I FURTHER DIRECT that as soon as hereafter possible, this Order be filed in the Office of the Secretary of State and that widespread publicity and notice be given of this Order.

sourceContexts.excerpt: Caltrans is directed to implement recommendations documented in the 2025 Transit Transformation Task Force Report to formalize the California Integrated Mobility Program through the following actions:

sourceNotices.excerpt: This Order is not intended to, and does not, create any rights or benefits, substantive or procedural, enforceable at law or in equity, against the State of California, its agencies, departments, entities, officers, employees, or any other person.

directives.label: 1(a)
directives.title: Statewide transit priority project list
directives.excerpt: develop a list of transit priority projects statewide that can be updated regularly

qualifiers.appliesTo: consistency with relevant local or regional transportation plans

timing.derivation: 120 calendar days after the effective date

directives.label: 1(b)
directives.title: State mobility-management role
directives.excerpt: Establish Caltrans as a Mobility Manager, as defined by the Federal Transit Administration

qualifiers.text: as defined by the Federal Transit Administration
qualifiers.appliesTo: Caltrans Mobility Manager role

timing.derivation: 120 calendar days after the effective date

directives.label: 1(c)
directives.title: Section 5307 direct-recipient option
directives.excerpt: Notify jurisdictions receiving FTA Section 5307 Urbanized Area Formula Program funding that Caltrans is available to serve as the direct recipient

qualifiers.text: if they elect to do so
qualifiers.appliesTo: jurisdictions choosing Caltrans as direct recipient

timing.derivation: 120 calendar days after the effective date

directives.label: 1(d)
directives.title: Service-led State Transit Plan
directives.excerpt: future iterations of the State Transit Plan shall adopt a service-led planning approach that aligns with the California State Rail Plan

qualifiers.appliesTo: State Transit Plan adoption of a service-led planning approach

timing.derivation: 120 calendar days after the effective date

directives.label: 1(e)
directives.title: Transit infrastructure design and permitting materials
directives.excerpt: Begin the process of developing design guidance for transit infrastructure on the state highway system

qualifiers.appliesTo: development of design guidance, standard plans and specifications, policy, and permitting procedures

timing.derivation: 120 calendar days after the effective date
one calendar-year anniversary of the effective date

directives.label: 1(f)
directives.title: CEQA transit-delivery report
directives.excerpt: Report to CalSTA how recent changes to the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) ... will be used to expedite transit projects

qualifiers.text: including but not limited to, Senate Bill 71 (Chapter 742, Statutes of 2025)
qualifiers.appliesTo: recent changes to CEQA covered by the report
use of recent CEQA changes to expedite transit projects

timing.derivation: 120 calendar days after the effective date

directives.label: 1(g)
directives.title: Active transportation delivery support team
directives.excerpt: Establish a Statewide Active Transportation Delivery Support Team

qualifiers.appliesTo: Active Transportation Program projects supported by the team
partnering with local and regional agencies to provide additional project delivery capacity

timing.derivation: 120 calendar days after the effective date

directives.title: Transit and rail funding dashboards
directives.excerpt: create real time dashboards that track the status of all available funding programs

qualifiers.appliesTo: the listed dashboard measures

qualifiers.text: as grant funding is consolidated into a system of record
qualifiers.appliesTo: addition of relevant performance measures

directives.label: 3(a)
directives.title: Federal formula-fund flexibility
directives.excerpt: Explore opportunities to support regions that reprogram Federal Highway Administration (FHWA) formula funds for transit uses

qualifiers.appliesTo: reprogramming FHWA formula funds for transit uses
matching state share dollars

directives.label: 3(b)
directives.title: Small-agency TDA and NTD reporting automation
directives.excerpt: Take steps to automate Transportation Development Act and National Transit Database reporting for rural and small agencies

qualifiers.appliesTo: Transportation Development Act and National Transit Database reporting
agencies covered by the reporting-automation action

directives.label: 3(c)
directives.title: Faster and more flexible fund distribution
directives.excerpt: Identify and implement measures to reduce the timelines for distribution of funds wherever feasible

qualifiers.appliesTo: reducing timelines for distribution of funds
lifting limits imposed on use of funds
providing pre-award authority for federal funds

directives.label: 3(d)
directives.title: Federal environmental-review assignment
directives.excerpt: Begin the process of obtaining a National Environmental Protection Act (NEPA) assignment from the U.S. Department of Transportation and Federal Transit Administration

qualifiers.appliesTo: obtaining the NEPA assignment

sourceNotes.text: The excerpt preserves the signed order's use of “National Environmental Protection Act (NEPA)” rather than silently changing the source wording.

directives.label: 3(e)
directives.title: BRT and bus-lane technical assistance
directives.excerpt: Provide technical assistance to local agencies, where feasible, with the implementation of Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) / Bus Only lanes

qualifiers.appliesTo: technical assistance to local agencies for BRT, bus-only lanes, and associated infrastructure

qualifiers.text: where possible via the cooperative agreement process
qualifiers.appliesTo: assistance with planning, engineering, and implementation

directives.label: 3(f)
directives.title: Digital real-property system, holdings digitization, and condition inventory
directives.excerpt: In coordination with the Department of General Services, create an implementation plan for the creation of a centralized, digital real property asset management system and fully digitize its real estate holdings, inventory buildings and their state of repair.

qualifiers.text: In coordination with the Department of General Services
qualifiers.appliesTo: the implementation plan, holdings digitization, and building repair-state inventory

directives.label: 3(g)
directives.title: Airspace policy for zero-emission fleets
directives.excerpt: Identify updates to its existing exception policy around the use of Caltrans airspace

qualifiers.text: where vehicles are already being stored
qualifiers.appliesTo: operator-controlled land beneath or adjacent to Caltrans facilities

qualifiers.text: where consistent with state policy and safety and operational requirements
qualifiers.appliesTo: airspace exception-policy updates

directives.label: 3(h)
directives.title: Excess toll-revenue guidance
directives.excerpt: provide guidance to toll operators on how to prioritize excess toll revenues for unmet transit operational and capital needs

qualifiers.text: in accordance with state and federal law
qualifiers.appliesTo: guidance on prioritizing excess toll revenues
Caltrans consideration of excess revenue for transit within planned managed-lane corridors

directives.label: 3(i)
directives.title: Transit updates to the California MUTCD
directives.excerpt: Initiate an update to the state Manual of Uniform Traffic Control Devices (MUTCD) to address transit infrastructure

qualifiers.appliesTo: transit infrastructure addressed by the MUTCD update, including transit signal priority and bus lanes
federal approvals for the MUTCD updates

directives.label: 3(j)
directives.title: Prohousing transit-funding priority
directives.excerpt: develop funding criteria that prioritize funding to, and award processing for, transit projects located in a jurisdiction with a Pro-Housing Designation

qualifiers.text: In consultation with the Department of Housing and Community Development and CTC
qualifiers.appliesTo: development of Pro-Housing funding and award-processing criteria

directives.title: CHSRA NEPA delegation and programmatic review
directives.excerpt: identify and implement opportunities to leverage CHSRA's Federal Railroad Administration (FRA) NEPA Delegation

qualifiers.appliesTo: undertaking programmatic environmental review

directives.title: State and federal funding support for transit priority and complete trips
directives.excerpt: CalSTA, in coordination with Caltrans and the CTC, shall update state funding program guidelines to incentivize the delivery of transit priority infrastructure and first- and last-mile connectivity including, but not limited to, Solutions for Congested Corridor Program (SCCP), the Active Transportation Program (ATP), Local Partnership Program (LPP), and Transit and Intercity Rail Capital Program (TIRCP). Caltrans shall also identify federal funding programs that will support transit priority and first and last-mile connectivity efforts.

qualifiers.text: in coordination with Caltrans and the CTC
qualifiers.appliesTo: CalSTA update of state funding program guidelines
state funding programs whose guidelines are to be updated

directives.title: Transit-data guidelines and grant CRM
directives.excerpt: publish guidelines for consistent transit and passenger rail data availability

qualifiers.text: implementing standards that allow for efficient integration between systems
qualifiers.appliesTo: guidelines for consistent transit and passenger rail data availability

qualifiers.text: ensuring that consistent information is available to the public
qualifiers.appliesTo: transit and passenger rail data availability

qualifiers.text: so that grantees receive streamlined, coordinated communications from a single source
qualifiers.appliesTo: implementation of the grant Customer Relationship Management system
